zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Barrett於大約1450年出生時，她的父親John Barrett of Bromley33歲，母親Margery Chichley12歲。她和Lord Thomas Baker至少有1個女兒。她去世於1535年10月31日在英國英格蘭肯特可藍別盧可享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Barrett于大约1450年出生时，她的父亲John Barrett of Bromley33岁，母亲Margery Chichley12岁。她和Lord Thomas Baker至少有1个女儿。她去世于1535年10月31日在英国英格兰肯特可藍別盧可享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barrett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): probably a nickname for a quarrelsome person, from Old French barat, Middle English bar(r)at, bar(r)et(te) ‘trouble, distress’, later ‘deception, fraud; contention, strife’. Through Norman settlement it also became common in Ireland, where it was Gaelicized as Baróid (Munster) and Baréid (Connacht).

variants.name: Barret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Matthieu, Mirlande, Philippe.

French: derivative of the topographic name   Barre   1 ‘barrier’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 3 below) is also found in Haiti.

variants.name: Baratta
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Pasquale, Alfonse, Antonio, Carmela, Guido, Salvatore, Vincenzo.
Italian: occupational name for a market trader, especially in northern Italy, from baratta ‘exchange, sale, buying and selling’, a derivative of barattare ‘to barter’. See also Barrett .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Barrett was born about 1450, her father, John Barrett of Bromley, was 33 and her mother, Margery Chichley, was 12. She had at least 1 daughter with Lord Thomas Baker. She died on 31 October 1535, in Cranbrook, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Joan Barrett est née environ 1450, son père, John Barrett of Bromley, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margery Chichley, avait 12 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lord Thomas Baker. Elle est décédée le 31 octobre 1535, à Cranbrook, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Joan Barrett um 1450 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Barrett of Bromley 33 und ihre Mutter Margery Chichley 12. Sie hatte mit Lord Thomas Baker mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 31. Oktober 1535 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Cranbrook, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Joan Barrett è nata il circa 1450, sua padre, John Barrett of Bromley, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Margery Chichley, aveva 12 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lord Thomas Baker. Morì il 31 ottobre 1535, nel Cranbrook, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Barrettが約1450年に生まれた時，父John Barrett of Bromleyは33歳，母Margery Chichleyは12歳であった。彼女はLord Thomas Bakerとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1535年10月31日にイギリスイギリスケントクランブルークで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Barrett(이)가 대략 1450년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Barrett of Bromley(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Chichley(은)는 12세였다. 그녀는 Lord Thomas Baker(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1535년 10월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 크랜브룩에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Joan Barrett родилась около 1450 г., её отцу по имени John Barrett of Bromley было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margery Chichley, 12.
